headline: USC's Offensive Mettle Tested Against Miami's Desperate Defense
subhead: The Trojans visit Hard Rock looking to stay unbeaten against a Hurricanes team searching for any sign of life.

xFactor.why: Miami's pass rush must generate early pressure to avoid falling behind to a USC offense that just survived Oklahoma's four-quarter fight.

keyMatchup: USC's ability to sustain drives against Miami's front seven will determine whether the Hurricanes can keep this competitive or spiral further.
body: USC arrives undefeated but not untested. The narrow five-point win over Oklahoma revealed a Trojans team capable of grinding out close games, though the offense occasionally stalled in the second half. Miami, meanwhile, has now lost consecutive games and sits among the nation's worst in scoring, managing only 17 points in a loss to Washington that exposed significant offensive struggles. The gap between a ranked team firing on most cylinders and one searching for rhythm is substantial.

The Hurricanes need their defense to dictate terms early, or this game gets away quickly. Miami's pass defense allowed 35 points to Washington in what amounted to a passing clinic. If USC's receivers find soft coverage and the Trojans establish their ground game, the margin could balloon past what a Miami offense in freefall can overcome. This is less about USC proving something and more about Miami avoiding further damage to an already fragile season.
